Jordan : Apakah kamu akan naik bus?

Ramli : Tidak, kami tidak. Kami pergi dengan mobil.

Jordan : Siapa yang akan mengemudikan mobil?

Ramli : Saya

Jordan : Jadi, kamu harus berhati-hati. ...


Pertanyaan 7Menjawab

A.
Jalan akan licin jika hujan


B.
Jalan akan licin jika hujan


C.
Jalan akan licin jika hujan.



D.
Jalan akan licin jika hujan

Pilihan jawaban A, B, C, dan D semuanya memiliki isi jawaban yang sama, yaitu "Jalan akan licin jika hujan." Oleh karena itu, dari informasi yang diberikan, tidak ada perbedaan di antara mereka, sehingga sulit untuk memilih jawaban yang paling tepat.

Namun, jika saya harus memilih, saya akan mengatakan jawaban yang tepat adalah:

A. Jalan akan licin jika hujan

Karena ini adalah peringatan yang masuk akal bagi seseorang yang akan mengemudi, ditujukan untuk mengingatkan untuk berhati-hati di jalan, khususnya dalam kondisi cuaca yang mungkin berubah menjadi buruk.

I was walking along the Main Street about 10 a.m. when I saw this blue car stop outside the bank. Next thing I saw was a man with a mask on. He.... out of the car and ran into the bank. I... walked up to the door of the bank and looked in. By this time, the robber had everyone in the bank lying on the floor. At this point, I hurried to the telephone box in Park Road and... the police.

Based on the context of the sentences provided, the most appropriate way to fill in the blanks would be:

I was walking along the Main Street about 10 a.m. when I saw this blue car stop outside the bank. Next thing I saw was a man with a mask on. He **jumped** out of the car and ran into the bank. I **quickly** walked up to the door of the bank and looked in. By this time, the robber had everyone in the bank lying on the floor. At this point, I hurried to the telephone box in Park Road and **called** the police.

The words "jumped," "quickly," and "called" fit grammatically and contextually, providing a coherent account of the events described.

I was walking along the Main Street about 10 a.m. when I saw this blue car stop outside the bank. Next thing I saw was a man with a mask on. He… out of the car and ran into the bank. I… walked up to the door of the bank and looked in. By this time, the robber had everyone in the bank lying on the floor. At this point, I hurried to the telephone box in Park Road and… the police.  What is the suitable verb to complete the text above?


Question 8Answer

a.
jumped



b.
yelled



c.
called



d.
then

To complete the text with the most suitable verbs based on the context of a bank robbery narrative, we would fill in the blanks as follows:

He **jumped** out of the car and ran into the bank. (Option a)
I **then** walked up to the door of the bank and looked in. (Option d)
At this point, I hurried to the telephone box in Park Road and **called** the police. (Option c)

The situation described involves an urgent and alarming action (a bank robbery), thus the verbs should reflect sudden and decisive actions:

- "jumped" indicates rapid movement out of the car,
- "then" is an adverb indicating the sequence of events but is needed to transition to the next part of the story,
- "called" indicates the action of contacting the authorities during an emergency.

The correct answers to the question would be:

a. jumped (first blank)
d. then (second blank)
c. called (third blank)

Andy : Saya dengar akan ada konser malam ini. Maukah kamu pergi bersamaku?
Sam : Sayangnya aku tidak bisa.
andi : kenapa? Menurutku kamu sangat menyukai band ini.
Sam : Ya, benar. Tapi aku punya banyak pekerjaan di rumah.
andi : kalau begitu biarkan aku membantumu.
Sam : ...

 

          Apa yang harus Sam katakan?


Pertanyaan 9Menjawab

A.
saya baik baik saja terima kasih



B.
Tidak, terima kasih. Saya bisa melakukannya sendiri



C.
Saya sangat menghargai bantuan Anda



D.
Saya tidak butuh bantuan Anda

Berdasarkan konteks percakapan, ketika seseorang menawarkan bantuan dan respons yang sopan dan berterima kasih disampaikan, Sam mungkin ingin menolak bantuan dengan cara yang sopan atau menerima tawaran bantuan tersebut. 

Jika ia ingin menolak (karena alasan pribadi, ingin bekerja sendiri, atau sebagainya), ia mungkin akan mengatakan:

B. Tidak, terima kasih. Saya bisa melakukannya sendiri

Jika ia ingin menerima tawaran bantuan dengan ucapan terima kasih, ia mungkin berkata:

C. Saya sangat menghargai bantuan Anda

Option A ("saya baik baik saja terima kasih") dan Option D ("Saya tidak butuh bantuan Anda") mungkin kurang cocok, karena A kurang jelas menanggapi tawaran bantuan dan D terdengar lebih keras dan mungkin tidak menyampaikan rasa terima kasih atas tawaran bantuan tersebut.

Jadi, jawaban yang paling cocok dan sopan tergantung pada bagaimana Sam ingin merespons adalah:

C. Saya sangat menghargai bantuan Anda

Cigarette smokers are said to have a good chance of getting various fatal diseases. But, I think that other people's tobacco smoke seems to increase the chances of non- smokers getting a wide range of cancers.

First, although passive smokers inhale less tobacco smoke, the researches point out that the smoke you breathe in is richer in many toxic chemicals. There is, for example, three times as much as benzo-apyrene, six times as much dimethyinitrosamine. Of course this substance will harm our body in the long run.

Secondly, the recent research reported that the risk of getting cancers not normally associated with smoking also rose among passive smokers. The risk of leukimia rose 6-8 times and the risk of cervical cancer increased 3-4 times.

Thirdly, past studies have found that the product of cigarette smoke such as coinine and thiocyanate, turn up in the blood, urine and saliva of non-smoking adults, children and fetuses that have been exposed to smokers. These substances are the main causes of various cancers.

In short, the effects of exposure to the cigarette smoking of others are greater than has been previously suspected. That is why passive smokers have more chances of. Who is a passive smoker?

A passive smoker is someone who inhales smoke from cigarettes, cigars, or pipes that is exhaled by others or comes from the burning end of tobacco products. They are often referred to as secondhand smokers or involuntary smokers. Passive smokers are exposed to the same harmful chemicals as active smokers, albeit typically in smaller quantities, but the exposure can still lead to an increased risk of various health issues, including respiratory problems and cancers, as mentioned in your text.

Mr. Bondy is a performer. His work is to make a joke and make people laugh. He usually
performs on a television program. He is so funny and makes other people feel happy.

  He is a ...

Based on the description provided, Mr. Bondy would be referred to as a comedian. A comedian is a performer whose work is to entertain by making jokes and causing laughter, often performing on television programs, in comedy clubs, theaters, or movies.

Guru : Semua siswa...datang ke sekolah pada pukul 06.00 pagi karena kita akan ada latihan.

Siswa : Iya, Bu!

         Modal apa yang cocok untuk mengisi bagian yang kosong?

Untuk mengisi bagian yang kosong dalam kalimat yang diberikan, modal yang cocok adalah "harus". Modal "harus" digunakan untuk menyatakan keharusan atau kewajiban.

Jadi, kalimat lengkapnya adalah:

Guru : Semua siswa harus datang ke sekolah pada pukul 06.00 pagi karena kita akan ada latihan.

Siswa : Iya, Bu!

He asked me, “Can I borrow your book for a moment?”

      What did he say?


Question 13Answer

a.
He asked me if he could borrow my book for a moment


b.
He asked me whether he can borrow my book for a moment


c.
He asked me whether I can borrow your book for a moment


d.
He asked me if he can borrow my book for a moment

When reporting speech in English, it is common to backshift the tense of the verbs. Therefore, "Can I borrow" would usually become "could I borrow" in reported speech. The correct answer is:

a. He asked me if he could borrow my book for a moment

This choice correctly changes "can" to "could," to reflect reported speech, and uses "if" to introduce the reported question.

Cindy  :  Feb, next month we are going to have an exam.

Febry         :  Yeah, we must study hard for the exam.

Cindy         :  I think so.

     The underlined sentence express ... .


Question 15Answer

a.
Opinion



b.
Prohibition



c.
Obligation



d.
Suggestion

The underlined sentence "Yeah, we must study hard for the exam" expresses an obligation. When someone uses "must" in this context, they are conveying something that is necessary or mandatory to do.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

c. Obligation
